signals.headline: BANK OF KOREA HOLDS RATE STEADY AS INFLATION RISES ABOVE TARGET AMID OIL PRICE SURGE | NO | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
BANK OF KOREA HOLDS RATE STEADY AS INFLATION RISES ABOVE TARGET AMID OIL PRICE SURGE | NO | HIGH confidence**

The most recent announcement comes from April 10, 2026, when the **Bank of Korea's Monetary Policy Board decided to keep its benchmark interest rate unchanged at 2.5% for the 7th consecutive month**[6]. This is the breaking development most likely to move prediction markets.

signals.headline: **Inflation is now above the BOK's 2% target**: Consumer prices have climbed above the central bank's two-percent target
signals.raw: **Inflation is now above the BOK's 2% target**: Consumer prices have climbed above the central bank's two-percent target, driven largely by surging oil costs[6]. The Policy Board expects inflation to rise to the mid to upper two-percent range[6].

signals.headline: **External risk acknowledged**: The BOK cited external risk factors (likely the Iran conflict mentioned in earlier repor
signals.raw: **External risk acknowledged**: The BOK cited external risk factors (likely the Iran conflict mentioned in earlier reports) as justification for maintaining the rate hold despite rising inflation pressures[6].
